summary: Split a large Dolphin AX assembly blob into AX subsystem-specific assembly files and replaced many anonymous func_/lbl_ symbols with names derived from external debug info. The PR states the labels and file splits were based on debug information from Jeremy McGrath Supercross World, which had perfect matches and may have used the same AX compiler/version, plus Sims 2 for functions missing from that source. The build was updated to include the new AX asm objects, and sysdolphin baselib callsites were relabeled to use the new AX symbol names.

key_files.role: Kept AX allocation/voice-stack code and removed most of the previous monolithic AX contents; relabeled symbols such as __AXStackHead, __AXStackTail, __AXCallbackStack, AXAcquireVoice, AXFreeVoice, AXSetVoicePriority, __AXPushFreeStack, __AXPopCallbackStack, and __AXRemoveFromStack.

New split file for AX auxiliary-buffer code, with named state such as __AXAuxDspWritePosition, __AXAuxDspReadPosition, __AXAuxCpuReadWritePosition, __AXAuxADspWrite, __AXAuxADspRead, __AXAuxBDspWrite, and __AXAuxBDspRead.
New split file for AX command-list-related assembly, including named AXCL globals such as __AXClMode.
New split file for AX output/AI callback code, including __AXOutNewFrame, __AXOutAiCallback, __AXOutBuffer, __AXOutFrame, __AXOutDspReady, __AXOsTime, and __AXDSPDoneFlag labels visible in the diff.
New split file for AX SPB code, including __AXSPBInit and SPB-related named globals.
New split file for AX VPB/voice-parameter-block code, including __AXVPBInit and __AXNumVoices.
New split file for AX profiling code, including __AXProfileInitialized.
New split file for AX DSP code/data, including axDspSlaveLength.
New split file for remaining assembly; the diff includes a note that this is likely not part of AX, preserving uncertain classification instead of forcing it into a named AX module.
Updated baselib synth callsites to the new AX names, for example AXAcquireVoice.
Updated AXDriver callsites to named AX functions, including AXRegisterAuxBCallback in the diff excerpt.
Added the new split AX asm objects: AXAux.s.o, AXCL.s.o, AXOut.s.o, AXSPB.s.o, AXVPB.s.o, AXProf.s.o, DSPCode.s.o, and ax_unk.s.o.

what_changed: The previous huge asm/dolphin/ax/AXAlloc.s file was reduced by about 8047 deleted lines and retained the allocation/voice-stack portion with meaningful AX labels.
New asm/dolphin/ax files were introduced for AXAux, AXCL, AXOut, AXSPB, AXVPB, AXProf, DSPCode, and ax_unk, totaling thousands of moved/split assembly lines.

Anonymous function names were replaced with Dolphin AX-style public/internal names, e.g. func_80358AE0 became __AXServiceCallbackStack, func_80358C44 became __AXPopCallbackStack, func_80358C60 became __AXRemoveFromStack, func_80358CF4 became AXFreeVoice, func_80358D74 became AXAcquireVoice, and func_80358EE8 became AXSetVoicePriority.
Anonymous data labels were replaced with named AX globals, e.g. lbl_804A8D78 became __AXStackHead, lbl_804A8DF8 became __AXStackTail, and lbl_804D74F0 became __AXCallbackStack.
Cross-file callsites in sysdolphin baselib assembly were updated to call the new names rather than func_/lbl_ placeholders.
obj_files.mk was extended so each newly split AX asm file builds as its own object in the Dolphin AX object list.
smart_moves: Used debug information from other GameCube titles as a high-signal source for SDK/library symbol names and file boundaries instead of guessing from Melee alone.
Separated mechanical file splitting from semantic labeling while keeping assembly bytes intact, making the AX library easier to navigate without requiring a C rewrite in this PR.
Preserved uncertainty by naming one residual file ax_unk.s and adding a comment that it is likely not part of AX, avoiding misleading module attribution.
Updated all visible callsites and build object lists in the same PR, preventing split-file symbols from becoming disconnected or unbuilt.
Applied Dolphin AX naming style consistently: public AX* functions, internal __AX* functions/globals, and module-prefixed globals such as __AXAux* and __AXOut*.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling SDK/library code, matching debug-info-bearing games can provide exact function names, global names, and file splits; cite the source titles and confidence level in the PR body.
Large assembly blobs can be profitably split by original library module before C decompilation, because module boundaries reduce search scope and make later matching work more targeted.
Relabeling data symbols is as valuable as relabeling functions: stack heads/tails, callback pointers, output frames, DSP flags, and aux buffer positions expose subsystem structure.
Do not force every nearby code range into the inferred library module; keep uncertain leftovers in an explicitly unknown file until better evidence exists.
After assembly splits, update both the build object list and all cross-module callsites so branch targets remain named and searchable.
naming_conventions: Dolphin AX exported functions use AX-prefixed public names such as AXAcquireVoice, AXFreeVoice, and AXSetVoicePriority.
Dolphin AX internal functions and globals use double-underscore __AX* names such as __AXAllocInit, __AXServiceCallbackStack, __AXStackHead, and __AXCallbackStack.
Module-specific internal globals carry their module in the name, e.g. __AXAuxDspWritePosition, __AXAuxCpuReadWritePosition, __AXOutFrame, __AXOutDspReady, and __AXClMode.
Unidentified residual assembly was named ax_unk.s rather than assigned a misleading formal AX module name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Port symbol names and file boundaries from debug-info-bearing external games only where the code appears to match; the PR body specifically cites Jeremy McGrath Supercross World for perfect matches and Sims 2 for missing functions.
Keep instruction streams unchanged while replacing func_ and lbl_ placeholders with named symbols so the binary match is preserved and the diff stays reviewable as a relabel/split.
Move contiguous ranges out of a monolithic asm file into separate module files and add each new file to obj_files.mk in original link order.
Use named data references in lis/addi and sda21 operands, e.g. __AXStackHead@ha/@l and __AXCallbackStack@sda21, to document global ownership without changing codegen.
Update branch/call operands after relabeling so downstream decomp can search for semantic names rather than addresses.
review_feedback: No inline review comments or textual reviewer feedback were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that convert the newly split asm/dolphin/ax files into C source files.
Search for ax_unk.s follow-up work to determine which non-AX subsystem the uncertain assembly range belonged to.
Search for uses of Jeremy McGrath Supercross World or Sims 2 debug info in other Dolphin SDK module labeling PRs.
Audit later AX header/struct PRs that rely on symbols introduced here, especially AXVPB, AXSPB, AXAux, and AXOut globals.
